data.description:
# OSS v1.0.0 community front door — contribution intake, governance, and release artifacts

## Context

rebar is technically near-1.0 (PyPI Trusted Publishing + PEP 740 attestations, immutable releases, `docs/api-stability.md` stability matrix, event-schema forward-compat) but the contribution model is closed: GitHub (navapbc/rebar) is a read-only mirror with Issues and Discussions disabled (verified via API 2026-07-09), and CONTRIBUTING.md assumes Nava-internal Gerrit onboarding. Three converging assessments (internal repo audit, an external deep-research report, and peer research across Go/Chromium/Qt/LibreOffice/Wikimedia/OpenStack/Eclipse) identify public intake + written governance as the only blockers to a credible v1.0.0.

Peer convergence establishes the design: KEEP Gerrit (Wikimedia reversed its GitLab exit in 2024 over the review model; JGit migrated TO GerritHub rather than accept PRs), pair the wall with a ramp (bot-answered PRs, a first-change tutorial, a shepherded-patch fallback), keep GitHub Issues as the zero-friction intake (Go's model), enforce DCO at the Gerrit server (OpenStack's 2025 CLA→DCO move), and skip non-convergent ceremony (CODEOWNERS, GPG-signed tags, RC trains — each an explicit recorded non-goal with rationale in a child).

Stars/forks/contributor counts are explicitly OUT of scope; success is artifact completeness and funnel quality, which are in our control.

## Success Criteria

- An outsider can: report a bug on GitHub in under 2 minutes; follow a documented path from GitHub to a merged Gerrit change; or submit a small patch with zero Gerrit setup (shepherded fallback with authorship preserved).
- Governance, review policy, DCO, and maintainer info are published, each grounded in a named peer convention (curl/CNCF, Chromium/Zuul, OpenStack/moby).
- Release artifacts (CHANGELOG, dependabot, README front door, 1.0 declaration mechanics) match 2026 Python-tool convergence (git-cliff generate-then-curate; github-actions-only dependabot; classifier bump; no tag-signing ceremony).
- Every open design decision recorded in a child is resolved and its outcome written back to the ticket before close.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All eight child stories closed: GitHub intake, PR posture, onboarding docs, DCO, governance docs, review policy, CHANGELOG, release hygiene / 1.0 declaration.
- [ ] The four pre-flight experiments (fork-side UX with has_pull_requests=false; Gerrit Forge Author ACL check; PR-event dependency audit of existing GitHub Actions; rebar-ticket trailer policy for shepherded patches) executed and outcomes recorded as ticket comments.
- [ ] The `docs/api-stability.md` pre-1.0 caveat flip and the Development Status classifier bump are staged for the v1.0.0 release change.
- [ ] No new artifact contradicts the Gerrit-primary workflow (CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, and the new docs stay mutually consistent).
data.title: OSS v1.0.0 community front door — contribution intake, governance, and release artifacts
